title: Open Widget Interface
text: Open Widget Interface Physical widgets for Form components are implemented using the Open Widget Interface (OWI). The OWI is an API that defines how widgets are included in Uniface components and how they interact with data. It is possible to incorporate other widgets into Uniface using the OWI. For example, the Uniface calendar widget is really an OWI-implemented wrapper around the Microsoft Windows calendar control. The OWI is available as a set of header files ( o*.h ) in the 3GL/include directory of the Uniface installation. Note: Although Unifields and labels are functionally equivalent to logical widgets, they are not true widgets, and are not addressed using the OWI. Note: The names of all physical widgets supplied with Uniface begin with the letter U, so avoid giving your custom widgets physical names beginning with U. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
